berlin.golden_bear.note: Documentary Golden Bear for Gianfranco Rosi's Fuocoammare / Fire at Sea — observational documentary about migrant rescue operations on the Italian island of Lampedusa. Rosi had previously won Venice's Golden Lion for documentary Sacro GRA in 2013, making him one of only a handful of directors to take top prizes at both Berlin and Venice for documentary work. The 2016 win marked the second documentary Golden Bear of the modern era and a continued Berlinale political-documentary streak.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 66th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Bosnian director Danis Tanović — Oscar winner for No Man's Land (2001) — takes the Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize for Death in Sarajevo, set on the centennial of the Archduke Franz Ferdinand assassination.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 66th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Mia Hansen-Løve's first Silver Bear for Best Director — French auteur honoured for the Isabelle Huppert vehicle Things to Come (L'Avenir). Hansen-Løve emerges as one of the most consistently prized European directors of the late 2010s.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 66th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: Tunisian actor Majd Mastoura — first-time performer recognised for his role in Hedi (Mohamed Ben Attia's debut feature). Same-film cross-honour: Hedi also won Best First Feature at the same ceremony.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 66th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: Danish actress Trine Dyrholm wins for Thomas Vinterberg's The Commune — recognised for her layered portrayal of a wife whose marriage is upended by her husband's open-marriage proposal in a 1970s Copenhagen commune.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 66th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Awarded for cinematography of Crosscurrent. Mark Lee Ping Bin (Mark Lee Ping Bing / Lee Ping-bin) is one of Asian cinema's most celebrated DPs — long-time collaborator of Hou Hsiao-hsien and the cinematographer of In the Mood for Love (uncredited 2nd-unit) and Café Lumière.
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 66th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.winner_film: A Lullaby to the Sorrowful Mystery
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.note: Filipino slow-cinema auteur Lav Diaz takes the Alfred Bauer Prize for his eight-hour A Lullaby to the Sorrowful Mystery — one of the longest films ever to play in any festival main competition. Diaz went on to win the Venice Golden Lion later the same year (2016) for The Woman Who Left, making 2016 his international breakout.
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.source: 66th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.best_first_feature.note: Tunisian director Mohamed Ben Attia's debut Hedi sweeps a Best First Feature + Best Actor double at cer 2016 — same-film, same-festival cross-category honour. Berlin's stamp helped propel the new wave of Tunisian arthouse cinema in the mid-2010s.
berlin.best_first_feature.source: 66th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2016.notes: 66th Berlinale — second-ever modern documentary Golden Bear (Gianfranco Rosi). Best Screenplay was NOT awarded at this ceremony (real-data gap, scraper correctly returns 0 rows; Phase D tolerance covers). Hedi takes a same-film Best Actor + Best First Feature double; Lav Diaz wins Alfred Bauer with an eight-hour film and goes on to take the Venice Golden Lion later in 2016.

berlin.golden_bear.note: Hungarian director Ildikó Enyedi's first Golden Bear — On Body and Soul is a slaughterhouse-set romance featuring shared deer dreams. The film went on to a 2018 Best Foreign Language Film Oscar nomination, making 2017 Enyedi's international breakout after a quarter-century gap from her 1989 Camera d'Or win for My Twentieth Century.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 67th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Senegalese-French director Alain Gomis — Kinshasa-set music drama.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 67th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Finnish auteur Aki Kaurismäki's Silver Bear for Best Director — second installment of his Refugee/Port trilogy. One of Kaurismäki's last announced features before his post-2017 'retirement' that he subsequently reversed.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 67th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: Austrian actor Georg Friedrich — long-time character actor finally winning a major festival prize for his lead role in Thomas Arslan's Bright Nights.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 67th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.winner_film: On the Beach at Night Alone
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: South Korean actress Kim Min-hee wins for Hong Sang-soo's On the Beach at Night Alone — a thinly-veiled autobiographical film about her relationship with the (married) director, which had been a Korean tabloid scandal. Hong-Kim collaboration recognition that pre-figures Hong's three-Silver-Bear Berlinale streak in cer 2021-2024.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 67th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.note: Sebastián Lelio (with Gonzalo Maza) takes Best Screenplay for A Fantastic Woman — film centred on a transgender protagonist (Daniela Vega). The film went on to win the 2018 Best Foreign Language Film Oscar, making cer 2017 Berlin its international launchpad.
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 67th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Awarded for editing of Ana, mon amour (Călin Peter Netzer). Dana Bunescu is one of Romanian New Wave cinema's signature editors (4 Months, 3 Weeks and 2 Days; Aurora; Toni Erdmann).
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 67th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.note: Polish veteran Agnieszka Holland (Europa Europa, In Darkness) takes Alfred Bauer for Spoor — eco-mystery thriller adapted from Olga Tokarczuk's novel.
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.source: 67th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.best_first_feature.note: Carla Simón's debut feature Estiu 1993 (Summer 1993) wins Best First Feature — Catalan-language autobiographical film. Simón returned to Berlin five years later (cer 2022) and won the Golden Bear for Alcarràs, completing a Berlin BFF → Golden Bear arc within the same festival.
berlin.best_first_feature.source: 67th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2017.notes: 67th Berlinale — Ildikó Enyedi returns after a 25-year hiatus; Hong Sang-soo / Kim Min-hee Berlinale connection that pre-figures Hong's later Silver Bear streak; A Fantastic Woman launches toward an Oscar. Carla Simón's BFF win here precedes her cer 2022 Golden Bear, making her a rare both-Berlin-debut-and-top-prize winner.

berlin.golden_bear.note: Adina Pintilie's debut feature Touch Me Not wins the Golden Bear AND Best First Feature at the same ceremony — extraordinarily rare same-film, same-director, same-festival double. Touch Me Not is a documentary-fiction hybrid about intimacy and disability. Pintilie joins the very short list of directors who won the Berlinale's top prize on a debut feature.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 68th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Polish director Małgorzata Szumowska's second Berlin Silver Bear (after her 2015 Best Director win for Body), confirming her as a Berlinale regular in the 2010s.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 68th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Wes Anderson's first major festival award for direction — Isle of Dogs is his second stop-motion animated feature. Notable that a stop-motion film took a competitive director Silver Bear; the Berlinale opened with Isle of Dogs that year.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 68th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: Young French actor Anthony Bajon — recovery-drama lead role.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 68th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: Paraguayan actress Ana Brun — late-career performer winning a major festival prize. The Heiresses takes an unusual same-film double at cer 2018, also winning the Alfred Bauer Prize for director Marcelo Martinessi.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 68th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.note: Mexican director-writer Alonso Ruizpalacios (with Manuel Alcalá) — Museum is a heist film starring Gael García Bernal. Ruizpalacios returns to Berlin's prize roster years later (cer 2021 OAC for editing of A Cop Movie).
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 68th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Awarded for costume and production design of Dovlatov (Aleksey German Jr.). Unusual dual-craft OAC citation — Berlin recognises both costume and production design as a unified contribution.
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 68th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.note: Paraguayan director Marcelo Martinessi — The Heiresses takes Alfred Bauer for director AND Best Actress (Ana Brun) at the same ceremony. Cer 2018 has TWO same-film cross-category doubles (Touch Me Not for Golden Bear + BFF; The Heiresses for Best Actress + Alfred Bauer) — the most concentrated cross-honour year in this batch.
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.source: 68th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.best_first_feature.note: Touch Me Not's second prize at cer 2018 — same-film, same-director Golden Bear + Best First Feature double. Permanent festival landmark.
berlin.best_first_feature.source: 68th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2018.notes: 68th Berlinale — landmark cross-honour ceremony. Touch Me Not (Adina Pintilie debut) takes Golden Bear + Best First Feature; The Heiresses (Marcelo Martinessi debut) takes Best Actress (Ana Brun) + Alfred Bauer. Wes Anderson's first festival Best Director Silver Bear.

berlin.golden_bear.note: Israeli director Nadav Lapid's Golden Bear for Synonyms — autobiographical Paris-set drama about an Israeli emigrant. Lapid went on to a controversial 2022 IFFI Goa jury president speech criticising The Kashmir Files; cer 2019 Berlin remains his major-festival peak.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 69th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: François Ozon's Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize for By the Grace of God (Grâce à Dieu) — based on real Catholic-Church abuse cases in Lyon. Ozon's first major Berlin prize after a long career.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 69th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Berlin School director Angela Schanelec's first Silver Bear for Best Director — her formal-cinema return. Schanelec returns four years later for Best Screenplay (cer 2023, Music) — two distinct Silver Bears in this batch window.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 69th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: Chinese actor Wang Jingchun wins for Wang Xiaoshuai's So Long, My Son. Same-film acting double at cer 2019 — co-star Yong Mei takes Best Actress. The last Berlinale before the COVID pandemic and the last cer with the gendered Best Actor + Best Actress structure paired on the same film.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 69th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: Chinese actress Yong Mei wins for the same film (So Long, My Son) as her Best Actor co-star Wang Jingchun — historic same-film cross-acting double. One of the very few Berlinales where a single film took both gendered acting Silver Bears.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 69th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.note: Co-writer pair: Italian director Claudio Giovannesi and journalist-author Roberto Saviano (with Maurizio Braucci, captured by scraper as primary pair). Saviano is best known internationally for Gomorrah (2008 book + film). Piranhas is a Naples youth-mafia drama.
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 69th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Awarded for Cinematography of Out Stealing Horses (Hans Petter Moland; Stellan Skarsgård lead) — Norwegian-Swedish-Danish co-production.
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 69th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.note: German director Nora Fingscheidt's debut System Crasher takes the LAST Alfred Bauer Prize before its retirement — the prize was discontinued in 2020 following revelations about Bauer's Nazi-era ties. Fingscheidt's win therefore holds permanent landmark status as the FINAL Alfred Bauer laureate.
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.source: 69th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2019.notes: 69th Berlinale — last pre-COVID festival. So Long, My Son takes a same-film Best Actor + Best Actress double (Wang Jingchun + Yong Mei). System Crasher (Nora Fingscheidt) is the FINAL Alfred Bauer laureate before the prize's retirement. Best First Feature was NOT awarded at this ceremony per the Wikipedia article (real-data gap, scraper correctly returns 0 rows; Phase D tolerance covers).

berlin.golden_bear.note: Iranian director Mohammad Rasoulof's Golden Bear for There Is No Evil — a four-part anthology examining capital punishment in Iran. Rasoulof was BANNED from leaving Iran by the regime and could not attend the ceremony; the prize was accepted in absentia. Rasoulof later went into exile and won the 2024 Cannes Special Jury Prize for The Seed of the Sacred Fig — making the cer 2020 Golden Bear part of one of the most-defining political-cinema arcs in modern festival history.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 70th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: American director Eliza Hittman's Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize — abortion-access road-trip drama. The film's pre-pandemic festival run was abruptly truncated by the March 2020 COVID lockdown; the Berlin Silver Bear remained its highest-profile recognition.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 70th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Hong Sang-soo's first Silver Bear for Best Director — pre-figures his three-Silver-Bear Berlin streak across cer 2021-2024 (Best Screenplay 2021, Grand Jury 2022, Grand Jury 2024). Cer 2020 is the start of Hong's most-decorated Berlinale era.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 70th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: Italian actor Elio Germano — LAST winner of the gendered Silver Bear for Best Actor before its retirement. Berlin announced post-cer-2020 that the gendered acting prizes would be replaced by gender-neutral Best Leading Performance + Best Supporting Performance starting at cer 2021. Germano therefore holds permanent landmark status as the FINAL Best Actor laureate.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 70th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: German actress Paula Beer — LAST winner of the gendered Silver Bear for Best Actress, in Christian Petzold's Undine (the first installment of his Petzold-Beer water-mythology series; Petzold returned to Berlin's prize roster in cer 2023 with Afire taking the Grand Jury Prize). Beer holds permanent landmark status as the FINAL Best Actress laureate.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 70th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.note: Italian D'Innocenzo brothers — captured as a single recipient by the scraper (split_co_recipients=false on Best Screenplay).
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 70th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Awarded for cinematography of DAU. Natasha — installment of Ilya Khrzhanovsky's controversial DAU project (decade-long immersive Soviet-research-institute reconstruction). German DP Jürgen Jürges (long-time Fassbinder and Haneke collaborator) recognised for shooting under the project's notoriously unconventional production conditions.
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 70th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.best_first_feature.note: Colombian-French director Camilo Restrepo's experimental debut.
berlin.best_first_feature.source: 70th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2020.notes: 70th Berlinale — final pre-pandemic in-person festival. THREE simultaneous lifecycle landmarks: (1) Mohammad Rasoulof's in-absentia Golden Bear under Iranian travel ban; (2) Elio Germano is the FINAL Best Actor; (3) Paula Beer is the FINAL Best Actress. Alfred Bauer Prize is ABSENT from cer 2020 — retired after the cer 2019 ceremony following revelations about Bauer's Nazi-era ties. Hong Sang-soo's first Silver Bear, beginning his 2020-2024 Berlinale streak.

berlin.golden_bear.winner_film: Bad Luck Banging or Loony Porn
berlin.golden_bear.note: Romanian filmmaker Radu Jude's first Golden Bear, awarded at the 71st Berlinale's COVID-restricted online edition (industry-only March 2021; public summer 2021). Jude later won Berlin Best Screenplay for Kontinental '25 (cer 2025) — same-festival prize-pair across two ceremonies.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 71st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Ryusuke Hamaguchi's Berlin Grand Jury Prize for Wheel of Fortune and Fantasy. Hamaguchi went on to win the 2022 Best International Feature Oscar for Drive My Car, making 2021 his international breakout year.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 71st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 71st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_leading_performa.note: FIRST winner of the gender-neutral Silver Bear for Best Leading Performance, introduced at the 71st Berlinale (cer 2021) replacing the gendered Best Actor + Best Actress. Maren Eggert holds permanent landmark status as the inaugural Best Leading Performance laureate.
berlin.silver_bear_best_leading_performa.source: 71st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_supporting_perfo.note: FIRST winner of the gender-neutral Silver Bear for Best Supporting Performance, introduced at the 71st Berlinale (cer 2021) alongside Best Leading Performance. Lilla Kizlinger holds permanent landmark status as the inaugural Best Supporting Performance laureate.
berlin.silver_bear_best_supporting_perfo.source: 71st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.note: Hong Sang-soo's Berlin Best Screenplay for Introduction. Hong is a Berlin perennial — he won the Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize the following year (cer 2022) for The Novelist's Film, and again in cer 2024 for A Traveler's Needs. Three consecutive Berlinale prizes in the 2021-2024 window is one of the most concentrated festival runs of any director.
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 71st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Awarded for editing of A Cop Movie (Mexican docu-fiction hybrid by Alonso Ruizpalacios).
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 71st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2021.notes: First COVID-restricted Berlinale, split across two-stage ceremony (industry March 2021, public summer 2021). First year of gender-neutral acting prizes. Alfred Bauer Prize newly retired (after 2019 revelations); not awarded cer 2021+. Best First Feature returned at the standard cadence.

berlin.golden_bear.note: Carla Simón's first Golden Bear — Catalan-language film by a Spanish female director. Alcarràs is a quiet family drama about a peach-farming family losing their land, marking a continued Berlinale preference for European art cinema with regional/agricultural settings.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 72nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Hong Sang-soo's second consecutive Berlinale prize (Best Screenplay 2021 → Grand Jury 2022) — same director winning two distinct main-competition Silver Bears in two years.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 72nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Claire Denis's first Silver Bear for Best Director — French auteur honoured for Both Sides of the Blade (Avec amour et acharnement) starring Juliette Binoche and Vincent Lindon.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 72nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_leading_performa.winner_film: Rabiye Kurnaz vs. George W. Bush
berlin.silver_bear_best_leading_performa.source: 72nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_supporting_perfo.note: Indonesian actress Laura Basuki — Berlin's second Best Supporting Performance laureate.
berlin.silver_bear_best_supporting_perfo.source: 72nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.winner_film: Rabiye Kurnaz vs. George W. Bush
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.note: Same-film double-laureate at cer 2022: Rabiye Kurnaz vs. George W. Bush won both Best Leading Performance (Meltem Kaptan) and Best Screenplay (Laila Stieler) — a same-ceremony cross-category honour.
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 72nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Cambodian director Rithy Panh (and collaborator Sarit Mang — scraper captures Panh as primary). Panh is best known for genocide-witness documentary work (The Missing Picture, Cannes 2013). Berlinale OAC awarded for the artistic contribution as a whole, not a single craft specialty.
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 72nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.best_first_feature.source: 72nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2022.notes: First post-COVID in-person Berlinale (held in February 2022 with reduced capacity). Hong Sang-soo's second consecutive Silver Bear; Carla Simón becomes a young female Golden Bear laureate; Rabiye Kurnaz takes a same-film double across acting + screenplay.

berlin.golden_bear.note: Documentary film wins the Golden Bear — On the Adamant is a French observational documentary set on a Seine river-boat psychiatric day-care centre. Documentary Golden Bears are rare; Nicolas Philibert (To Be and to Have, 2002) had previously been a celebrated documentarian without major festival hardware. The 2023 win signalled the Berlinale's continued openness to non-fiction in the main competition.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 73rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Christian Petzold's first Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize (after multiple Berlin nominations). Afire (Roter Himmel) is part of Petzold's elemental-themed trilogy with Paula Beer.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 73rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Veteran French director Philippe Garrel's first Berlin Best Director (his fourth-decade festival recognition). The Plough is a family drama featuring three of Garrel's own children.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 73rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_leading_performa.note: Sofía Otero — at age 8 — became the youngest winner of a Berlin acting prize in festival history, taking the Silver Bear for Best Leading Performance for her portrayal of a transgender child in 20,000 Species of Bees. Notable both for the actor's age and for the gender-neutral acting category enabling cross-age recognition.
berlin.silver_bear_best_leading_performa.source: 73rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_supporting_perfo.winner_film: Till the End of the Night
berlin.silver_bear_best_supporting_perfo.note: Thea Ehre — first openly transgender performer to win a Berlin acting Silver Bear, in the third year of the gender-neutral category. Confirms the category's intent to recognise performance regardless of gender.
berlin.silver_bear_best_supporting_perfo.source: 73rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.note: German Berlin School director Angela Schanelec — Music is a loose retelling of Oedipus.
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 73rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Awarded for cinematography of Disco Boy. Hélène Louvart is one of the most celebrated arthouse cinematographers active today (collaborator of Alice Rohrwacher, Maggie Gyllenhaal, Wim Wenders).
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 73rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.best_first_feature.note: Co-directed Argentinian docu-fiction hybrid (single bullet, two named co-directors).
berlin.best_first_feature.source: 73rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2023.notes: 73rd Berlinale — documentary Golden Bear (rare); Sofía Otero (age 8) becomes youngest Berlin acting laureate; Thea Ehre is first transgender Berlin acting Silver Bear winner.

berlin.golden_bear.note: Mati Diop's Golden Bear for Dahomey — Senegalese-French director's documentary on the return of looted artefacts from France to Benin. Diop's prior feature Atlantics had won the Cannes Grand Prix in 2019; Dahomey makes her one of very few directors to have won major top/second prizes at both Cannes and Berlin. Confirmed in Phase 1 bug fix audit (cer 2024).
berlin.golden_bear.source: 74th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Hong Sang-soo's THIRD Berlinale main-competition prize in four years (Best Screenplay 2021 → Grand Jury 2022 → Grand Jury 2024). Confirmed in Phase 1 bug fix audit (cer 2024 grand jury film_title parser bug).
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 74th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.winner_recipient: Nelson Carlo De Los Santos Arias
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Dominican director Nelson Carlo De Los Santos Arias's Silver Bear for Best Director — Pepe is a magical-realist fable narrated from the perspective of a hippo descended from those introduced to Colombia by Pablo Escobar.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 74th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_leading_performa.note: Sebastian Stan's first major-festival acting prize. Stan is best known for the Marvel Cinematic Universe (Bucky Barnes / Winter Soldier) and the 2024 Trump biopic The Apprentice; A Different Man positioned him as a serious arthouse-acting voice in the same year.
berlin.silver_bear_best_leading_performa.source: 74th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_supporting_perfo.note: Emily Watson — long-celebrated British actress (Breaking the Waves, 1996) finally winning a Berlin Silver Bear after a three-decade career. Awarded for her supporting role in Small Things Like These (Cillian Murphy lead, Tim Mielants directing) — a Magdalene-laundries drama set in 1985 Ireland.
berlin.silver_bear_best_supporting_perfo.source: 74th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 74th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Awarded for cinematography of The Devil's Bath (Veronika Franz / Severin Fiala). The Devil's Bath is a folk-horror set in 18th-century Austria; Gschlacht's painterly cinematography was the explicit citation.
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 74th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.best_first_feature.source: 74th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2024.notes: 74th Berlinale — Mati Diop's Cannes-Berlin prize-pair across two festivals; Hong Sang-soo's third Silver Bear in four years; Sebastian Stan transitions to arthouse credibility; Emily Watson finally collects a Berlin prize after decades of acclaimed work.

berlin.golden_bear.note: Norwegian director Dag Johan Haugerud's Golden Bear for Dreams (Sex Love) — middle film of his Sex / Love / Dreams trilogy, with Berlin awarding the Dreams (Sex Love) installment. First Norwegian Golden Bear since 1979.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 75th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Brazilian director Gabriel Mascaro's near-future-set Amazonian fable.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 75th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Chinese director Huo Meng — rural-China multi-generational drama.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 75th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_leading_performa.winner_film: If I Had Legs I'd Kick You
berlin.silver_bear_best_leading_performa.note: Australian actress Rose Byrne's first major festival acting prize. If I Had Legs I'd Kick You is a Mary Bronstein psychological drama; Byrne plays a stressed mother in a chamber-drama tour de force.
berlin.silver_bear_best_leading_performa.source: 75th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_supporting_perfo.note: Andrew Scott (Fleabag, All of Us Strangers) wins for Blue Moon — Richard Linklater's Lorenz Hart biopic. Scott's first major festival acting Silver Bear.
berlin.silver_bear_best_supporting_perfo.source: 75th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.note: Radu Jude's second Berlin main-competition prize after his cer 2021 Golden Bear (Bad Luck Banging or Loony Porn) — same-festival prize-pair across two ceremonies, joining Hong Sang-soo as a Berlin perennial in this batch window.
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 75th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Lucile Hadžihalilović and the creative ensemble of The Ice Tower — Berlin frames this as ensemble craftsmanship rather than a single specialty (no parenthetical specialty in the wikitext, unlike most OAC bullets). Hadžihalilović is the French art-cinema director of Innocence and Earwig.
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 75th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.best_first_feature.winner_film: The Devil Smokes (and Saves the Burnt Matches in the Same Box)
berlin.best_first_feature.note: Mexican debut feature with one of the longest film titles in Berlinale Best First Feature history.
berlin.best_first_feature.source: 75th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2025.notes: 75th Berlinale — anniversary edition. Norway's first Golden Bear in over four decades; Radu Jude returns to the Berlinale prize roster four years after his Golden Bear; OAC unusually framed as ensemble-craft (no parenthetical specialty).
